items.title: Multiple Return Statements
items.summary: An argument for using multiple return statements in a method (as opposed to adhering to the single return myth).

items.title: Don't Remove Listeners - Use ListenerHandles
items.summary: Keeping references around to remove listeners is a hazard. ListenerHandles encapsulate the complexity and LibFX has an implementation.

items.title: LibFX 0.2.0 Released
items.summary: Release post for LibFX 0.2.0 including and pointers to GitHub, Feature descriptions, Maven coordinates and the Javadoc.

items.title: JavaFX Sources in Eclipse
items.summary: A quick step by step guide how to use the JavaFX sources in Eclipse by attaching them to the current JDK.

items.title: Impulse: "Lambdas In Java: A Peek Under The Hood"
items.summary: Discussing the talk "Lambdas in Java: A peek under the hood" given by Brian Goetz at the goto; conference 2013 in Aarhus.

items.title: Serialize Optional
items.summary: A summary of why you can't serialize Optional and what can be done to deal with that limitation if necessary.

items.title: The Serialization Proxy Pattern
items.summary: A presentation of the Serialization Proxy Pattern as defined in Effective Java. It defines the pattern, describes its implementation and gives examples.

items.title: Concepts of Serialization
items.summary: A close look at serialization and a presentation of some key concepts of Java's serialization system.

items.title: Why Isn't Optional Serializable?
items.summary: Discussing the reasons for not making Java 8's new type Optional serializable.

items.title: The Design of Optional
items.summary: A digest of how Optional was introduced in Java 8, summarizing the many discussions about it and their key points based on the mail archive of JSR-335.

items.title: Intention Revealing Code With Optional
items.summary: Write intention revealing code with Java 8's new type Optional and prevent most NPEs. This is not optional!

items.title: Impulse: "Architecture - The Lost Years"
items.summary: Discussing the talk "Architecture - The Lost Years", which Robert C. Martin held on several occasions.

items.title: The Decorator Pattern With Default Methods
items.summary: Use Java 8's default methods to make the decorator pattern even more beautiful, which results in more concise and readable code.

items.title: Impulse: "Workflows of Refactoring"
items.summary: Discussing the keynote "Workflows of Refactoring" by Martin Fowler at OOP 2014, where he categorizes different reasons for and ways of refactoring.

items.title: How The Decorator Pattern Saved My Day
items.summary: A real-life example how the decorator pattern enables future changes and improves code quality by upholding the Single Responsibility Principle.

items.title: CodeFX Up And Running
items.summary: Summer recess is officially over and shit got done! Finally, CodeFX is ready to take on the world.

items.title: LibFX 0.1.1 Released
items.summary: Release post for LibFX 0.1.1 including a description of Nestings and pointers to GitHub, Maven and the Javadoc.

items.title: Foobar
items.summary: A foobar post that may accidentally teach you about Foobar.
